message:
fix(channel): preserve reorder buffer pre-allocation across flushes

FaultSender::flush() previously used mem::take() to drain the reorder
buffer, which replaces it with a default-capacity (0) Vec. Subsequent
sends would then trigger reallocation as the buffer grows back to its
configured reorder_buffer_size.

This commit replaces mem::take() with mem::replace(..., Vec::with_capacity(config.reorder_buffer_size))
so the fresh buffer starts at the expected capacity, maintaining a
stable allocation profile across repeated flush cycles.

Also fixes a rustfmt line-break in record_duplication()'s fetch_add chain.

New test: flush_reestablishes_reorder_buffer_preallocation — verifies
the buffer capacity after flush is at least config.reorder_buffer_size.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
